body:
Disables the forward-compatible CUDA driver (basically, it will insist that we always used the system one).

This appears to fix the segfaults we were seeing, and _may_ fix some of the other errors as well.

title: Make IntervalTopology(mesh) pick up ClimaComms.device()

body:
This upgrades to HDF5_jll 1.14. As it is now built against MPI, this means some stuff breaks with our existing setup.

So I'm trying out using [MPItrampoline](https://github.com/eschnett/MPItrampoline). The key advantage is that all the binaries should now "just work". The downside is that we need to build [MPIwrapper](https://github.com/eschnett/MPIwrapper) around the MPI library.

title: Bump version

body:
Fixes #1120 and #1467.

I've gone with option 2 in https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaCore.jl/issues/1120#issuecomment-1737903200.

In the following benchmark:
```julia
using ClimaComms

import ClimaCore:
    ClimaCore,
    slab,
    Spaces,
    Domains,
    Meshes,
    Geometry,
    Topologies,
    Spaces,
    Fields,
    Operators


FT = Float64
vertdomain = Domains.IntervalDomain(
    Geometry.ZPoint{FT}(0.0),
    Geometry.ZPoint{FT}(1.0);
    boundary_tags = (:bottom, :top),
)
vertmesh = Meshes.IntervalMesh(vertdomain, nelems = 10)
vert_center_space = Spaces.CenterFiniteDifferenceSpace(vertmesh)

horzdomain = Domains.SphereDomain(30.0)
horzmesh = Meshes.EquiangularCubedSphere(horzdomain, 4)
horztopology = Topologies.Topology2D(
    ClimaComms.SingletonCommsContext(ClimaComms.CPUSingleThreaded()),
    horzmesh,
)
quad = Spaces.Quadratures.GLL{3 + 1}()
horzspace = Spaces.SpectralElementSpace2D(horztopology, quad)

hv_center_space =
    Spaces.ExtrudedFiniteDifferenceSpace(horzspace, vert_center_space)

coords = Fields.coordinate_field(hv_center_space)
x = Geometry.UVWVector.(cosd.(coords.lat), 0.0, 0.0)


t = ntuple(i -> x, 80);
f(t) = t
@time f(t);
```

On main:
```julia
julia> @time f(t);
  1.455831 seconds (343 allocations: 24.953 KiB, 100.00% compilation time)
```
on this branch
```julia
julia> @time f(t);
  0.002401 seconds (343 allocations: 24.953 KiB, 99.12% compilation time)
```
